verses.text: 'I love the LORD, because he hath heard my voice,' and so he will call on Him as long as he lives. Answered prayer kindles love. The God who hears us wins the heart's devotion.
'I love the LORD, because he hath heard my voice,' and so he will call on Him as long as he lives. Answered prayer kindles love. The God who hears us wins the heart's devotion.

When 'the sorrows of death compassed me' and grief and pain found him, he called on the name of the LORD to deliver his soul. Trouble drove him to prayer. In death's shadow the believer's instinct is to cry to God.

When 'the sorrows of death compassed me' and grief and pain found him, he called on the name of the LORD to deliver his soul. Trouble drove him to prayer. In death's shadow the believer's instinct is to cry to God.

The LORD is gracious, righteous, and merciful, preserving the simple; so he tells himself, 'Return unto thy rest, O my soul,' for God has dealt bountifully. Mercy restores his peace. The delivered soul is called back to quiet rest in God.

The LORD is gracious, righteous, and merciful, preserving the simple; so he tells himself, 'Return unto thy rest, O my soul,' for God has dealt bountifully. Mercy restores his peace. The delivered soul is called back to quiet rest in God.

The LORD is gracious, righteous, and merciful, preserving the simple; so he tells himself, 'Return unto thy rest, O my soul,' for God has dealt bountifully. Mercy restores his peace. The delivered soul is called back to quiet rest in God.

God has freed his soul from death, his eyes from tears, his feet from falling, that he may 'walk before the LORD in the land of the living.' Deliverance is for service. Saved from death, the believer lives to walk with God.

God has freed his soul from death, his eyes from tears, his feet from falling, that he may 'walk before the LORD in the land of the living.' Deliverance is for service. Saved from death, the believer lives to walk with God.
'I believed, therefore have I spoken,' though sorely afflicted he had said 'in my haste, All men are liars.' Faith spoke even amid distress. Trust may stammer hasty words, yet still it holds to God.
'I believed, therefore have I spoken,' though sorely afflicted he had said 'in my haste, All men are liars.' Faith spoke even amid distress. Trust may stammer hasty words, yet still it holds to God.

'What shall I render unto the LORD for all his benefits?' He will take 'the cup of salvation' and pay his vows. Gratitude seeks expression. The best return for mercy is to receive more grace and serve God openly.

'What shall I render unto the LORD for all his benefits?' He will take 'the cup of salvation' and pay his vows. Gratitude seeks expression. The best return for mercy is to receive more grace and serve God openly.

'What shall I render unto the LORD for all his benefits?' He will take 'the cup of salvation' and pay his vows. Gratitude seeks expression. The best return for mercy is to receive more grace and serve God openly.

'Precious in the sight of the LORD is the death of his saints'; owning himself God's servant, he rejoices that his bonds are loosed. God values His people's lives and deaths. Each saint's passing is precious in His eyes.

'Precious in the sight of the LORD is the death of his saints'; owning himself God's servant, he rejoices that his bonds are loosed. God values His people's lives and deaths. Each saint's passing is precious in His eyes.

He will offer 'the sacrifice of thanksgiving,' calling on the LORD and paying his vows in the courts of His house before all the people. Thanks become public worship. Mercy received is best repaid in open praise among God's people.
